rows.notes: Large headed script at top of page is legible as a title. The surname 'S.C. Jones' is fairly clear; the Latinized 'Computus Do Rectoris' reading is slightly uncertain in one small letter but overall clear.
Date line is clear in Roman/Latin script; numerals '1831' are distinct and alignment shows this is a header line.
Latin phrase and year '1832' readable though a couple of small flourishes ('revolutionem') are slightly ambiguous; clearly a continuation header line.

Centered section heading meant to read 'Arrearages recepta' (arrearages received). The second word is fairly clear as 'recepta' but the first word has an unusual flourish; chosen reading based on context.
Clear English heading in the body of the page. All letters legible.

Amount '40 0 0' is unambiguous and columns align well. The place-name is uncertain in spelling (handwritten capital M with unclear internal letters); chosen reading 'Munkernmot' approximates the script.
Description 'Kidlington' and ditto 'Do' are clear. Amount columns give '20 0 0' clearly aligned; validation rules satisfied.
Amount '2 15 0' is clear. Handwritten names read as 'Coxon and Brown' but first name's letters are slightly ambiguous (could be 'Copson' etc.); chosen 'Coxon' as best fit to script and spacing.

Amount appears as '1 12 4½' with a small loop after the pence digit interpreted as the halfpenny symbol ('ob'). Column alignment is consistent, but the tiny fraction mark is faint, hence moderate confidence.
Description 'Vicarage 1 year' clear. Amount '4 0 0' aligned and unambiguous.

Amount '9 6 8' is readable. The place-name appears as 'Sad Lane' (or 'Lad Lane') followed by '& Norris' — the initial letter is faint and could be L or S; reading chosen by best visual match but description is uncertain.
Amount '1 13 4' is well aligned and clear. The place-name looks like 'Rickford' though the middle letters have small flourishes; moderate confidence for the transcribed name.
Amount '6 13 4' clear. The descriptive text includes a date 'to March 1830' which is legible; the parish name is read as 'Sherenham' though a couple of letters are not crisp.
Amount '1 6 1' clear. The place text appears as 'Little New Becks' (letters N and w are slightly cramped); selected reading fits the script and context though some letters are ambiguous.
Description and amount '20 0 0' are both clearly written and well aligned with the currency columns.
Centered subsection heading 'Redeemed Land Tax' is clearly written and separated from previous entries.
Amount '22 5 6' is clear and well aligned. 'Kidlington Rectory' is legible; format matches earlier usage of the name.
Amount '28 0 0' very clear. 'Long Whittenham' reading is legible; 'Do.' indicates ditto and is standard.
Amount '14 0 0' clear. 'Yarnton' is a common parish name and the script matches that reading; alignment is good.
Underlined final total at bottom right reads '171 16 3½' — the halfpenny loop is visible. Columns used to align pounds/shillings/pence are unambiguous and values validate correctly.
